Faber2026 git operations (2026-07-08): DR8/DR9 resolution merged, draft-scaffolding prose merged, branch reconciled, WIP + scintillation campaign committed & merged (PR #34), leftover housekeeping

## Context carried in
Repo `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026` (rw grant), Overleaf-synced ApJ manuscript (CHIME/FRB–DSA-110 co-detected FRBs, 12 bursts). Submodule `pipeline` → `https://github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS.git`. Git author identity passed via env vars `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Jakob Faber" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="jfaber@caltech.edu"` (+ GIT_COMMITTER_*) because `.git/config` is write-protected in sandbox. `gh` CLI not installed → all PR ops via GitHub REST API with `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `User-Agent:"faber2026-agent"`. The recurring `fatal: failed to get/store: -50` and `could not write config file .git/config: Operation not permitted` are cosmetic keychain/config-write failures, NOT operation failures. Sandbox constraint discovered mid-chunk: superproject `.git/objects`+`.git/refs` are WRITABLE (commits succeed via plumbing), but the SUBMODULE store `.git/modules/pipeline/{objects,refs}` and all of `.git/modules/` is BLOCKED (`Operation not permitted`) — cannot commit inside submodule from this sandbox.

## Task 3 completion: DR8/DR9 conflict + cluster-catalog (start of chunk)
Agent confirmed via pipeline docs (`pipeline/docs/rse/specs/reproducibility-foreground-galaxies.md`) that DR8/DR9 is **not a conflict** but two deliberate stages: **discovery** cone search uses DESI Legacy DR8 North (VizieR `VII/292/north`); **validation** independently queries LS DR9 photo-z. Cluster `ClusterEngine` genuinely queries PSZ2/MCXC/MCXC-II (`config.py:81–83`), and "every matched cluster comes from the optical catalog" correctly reports only Wen & Han 2024 survived at declination — accurate, **cluster-catalog flag retracted as over-read, no edit**. Fix applied: `sections/observations.tex:105` discovery paragraph got "DR8 North" inserted ("DESI Legacy Imaging Surveys DR8 North photometric-redshift catalog"). Committed `4770820` ("Make discovery-stage Legacy release explicit (DR8 North)"), pushed to branch `docs/gate-f-readability-pass` (`81a255c..4770820`). Braces 128/128. Updated audit artifact `language_audit.md` (artifact_id `a1464fe3-dfcf-4171-a3e1-6f5df7e0d0db`) to v3 (version_id `5a65ceb8-75d5-4864-bac8-79638c169fe3`) retracting cluster item and marking DR8/DR9 resolved.

## USER e17: "Agreed, got ahead and push to origin/main or oversee a PR, review and merge so I can pull in the changes on Overleaf and recompile"

Agent discovered branch `docs/gate-f-readability-pass` had **diverged** from `origin/main`: 9 commits ahead / 12 behind (later 14 behind). The 9 ahead included figure pins, `.npz` binaries, zach C2D4 jointmodel figure, and a stale submodule pointer — NOT wanted on main. `origin/main` had advanced with merged PRs #26 (purge draft-status/nicknames), #27 (jointmodel refit panels), #28 (PR27 feedback), #30 ("Polish manuscript terminology"), plus Overleaf sync merges. Verified agent's 4 fixes were genuinely NOT yet on main (5 SLOTs still uncommented, "deep-learning estimator", WISE--PanSTARRS--STRM names, no DR8 label). Worktree approach blocked (`.git/worktrees` write-protected). Cherry-pick/merge-tree conflicted because main and branch had different SLOT wording ("dispersion-measure budget decomposition" on main vs "dispersion-budget decomposition" on branch). 

Solution: transform main's blobs directly via python text substitution, build commit via plumbing (`git hash-object -w`, `read-tree` into `GIT_INDEX_FILE=/tmp/f2026.index`, `update-index --cacheinfo`, `write-tree`, `commit-tree`). Note: `git` works in bash but FAILS in the `python` kernel (`fatal: unable to access '/Users/jakobfaber/.gitconfig': Operation not permitted`) — all git plumbing done in bash, python3 heredocs only for text transforms. Old bash lacks `declare -A` (associative arrays fail silently). Built commit `a3ca960779f10f88457bed5ba40880a419cfc967` on `origin/main` (5 SLOTs commented, classifier→PS1-STRM ×2, deep-learning→random-forest, DR8 North), pushed branch `fix/gate-f-slots-terminology`, opened **PR #31** (https://github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pull/31), mergeable/clean, both Socket Security checks success, **squash-merged → `b8e12c6a95e93b08468da9200cc7c408251c1b50`**. Verified on origin/main: 0 uncommented SLOT, DR8 North, random-forest, PS1-STRM lines 108+139. Remote branch deleted.

## USER e60: "Pulled into overleaf successfully. Proceed with the two follow-ups now."
Two follow-ups from prior turn: (1) remaining draft-scaffolding phrasing; (2) reconcile local branch.

### Follow-up 1: draft-scaffolding prose → PR #33
Found scaffolding phrases still on main across 5 files (rendered, not `%`-commented). Read exact contexts, built spliced blobs from main, applied register rewrites via python3 (one-writer, assert single-occurrence). Rewrites (verbatim old→new):
- `observations.tex`: "quantities are citable" → "reported"; "deferred pending completion of the scattering analysis (Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha})" → "presented in Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha}"
- `methods.tex`: "a trusted per-band amplitude fit" → "a well-constrained per-band amplitude fit"
- `results.tex`: "already informative as a validation step, even before the turbulence parameters are restored as citable measurements" → "provides a validation of the joint-fit morphology independent of the turbulence-parameter values themselves"; "the current FRB~20220310F" → "the FRB~20220310F"; "eleven current joint-model morphology-audit panels" → "eleven joint-model morphology-audit panels"; "we do not yet quote" → "we do not quote"
- `budget.tex`: "deferred pending completion of the scattering analysis" → "presented with the scattering analysis (Section~\ref{sec:results-alpha})"; "current rows with missed sub-components remain diagnostic only until their higher-multiplicity fits pass the same check" → "a fit with missed sub-components is treated as diagnostic and re-fit at higher multiplicity until it passes the same check"
- `appendix.tex`: "the current joint-model morphology audit" → "the joint-model morphology audit"

First commit attempt (`b9900197ca928aa5572fd68b94df720476171030`) only captured appendix.tex due to `declare -A` failure; rebuilt with explicit paths → `7e234035e9215b6c686ff9b2ee0760045eed2225` (5 files, +16/−17). Remaining "citable" hits all in `%` TODO comments (results.tex:177/183/206) — left as-is. Braces balanced. Pushed branch `fix/gate-f-scaffolding-prose`, opened **PR #33**, mergeable/clean, both Socket Security checks success, **squash-merged → `15bf9e955a6e3a4f1c4c3c1e2b12602f1a212e47`**. Verified 0 rendered scaffolding on origin/main. Remote branch deleted.

### Follow-up 2: branch reconcile → deletion + WIP fix (via ask_user)
Determined branch `docs/gate-f-readability-pass` **fully superseded**: all 9 commits' content already on main as identical blobs (zach C2D4 figure `zach_jointmodel_pair.pdf` blob `55f1cb22e99ba7a99276546fb338ccff679e25bd` identical both sides; all 6 zach C2D4 fit_artifacts identical); main has MORE (bib fix `25c8ca7`, casey C2D1 artifacts=6 on main/0 on branch, newer association-card renders, newer submodule pointer). Branch submodule pointer `92b4fdf906f8be0c83d17dd815fe684bee01ef7b` vs main `9017707e254672c363300755a013081412359a79`.

**HAZARD identified:** uncommitted working-tree WIP (a live DSA-110 scintillation ACF campaign) sits on the stale branch and its `results.tex`/`appendix.tex` still carried the OLD phrasing #31/#33 removed — committing as-is would revert merged fixes. WIP inventory: modified `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/rse/board/readiness.html`, `docs/rse/journal.jsonl`, `pipeline` (submodule), `scripts/journal-cadence-cursor-hook.sh`, `scripts/journal-cadence-posttool-hook.sh`, `scripts/journal-staleness-hook.sh`, `sections/appendix.tex`, `sections/results.tex`; untracked: `REPRODUCE.md`, `docs/referee_report_2026-07-07.md`, `docs/rse/specs/handoff-2026-07-08-08-55-open-author-decisions.md`, `docs/rse/specs/runbook-overleaf-propagation-2026-07-08.md`, `figures/dsa_lorentzian_summary.{pdf,png}`, `figures/dsa_scint_acf/` (12 `*_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.pdf`: casey, chromatica, freya, hamilton, isha, johndoeII, mahi, oran, phineas, whitney, wilhelm, zach — **12 not 13**, agent self-corrected count in e123), `language_audit.md`, `repro_manifest.csv`, `sections/dsa_scint_acf.tex`.

WIP backed up → `wip_backup_2026-07-08.tar.gz`, artifact_id `2ea75b4e-c655-4e22-81a8-d6648dd98f20`, version_id `4c37ac22-ef4a-4c53-8d5e-78dc518ce09a` (contents: wip_tracked.patch, results.tex.wip, appendix.tex.wip, REPRODUCE.md, untracked_list.txt).

**ask_user (e105/e106)** — question "The branch is fully superseded by main. How should I reconcile it, given your uncommitted scintillation WIP sits on top of it?" — user chose **"Delete branch + re-apply the 2 language fixes into WIP"**. Agent re-applied the 4 rewrites (results.tex lines 191–193, 195–196, 199–200 and appendix.tex line 143) via `edit_file`, verified old phrasing gone from rendered text (only `%`-comment "citable" remain), scintillation WIP intact (dsa_scint_acf.tex present, braces results 86/86 appendix 131/131). **Deleted remote branch** `docs/gate-f-readability-pass`. Local branch could not be deleted (checked out, holding WIP).

## Auditor e122 (finding at starts=166) + e123
Warned agent said "13 Lorentzian-fit figures" but untracked_list shows exactly 12. Agent acknowledged: "it's 12 `dsa_scint_acf/*_lorentzian_fits.pdf` files (one per burst), not 13. The `dsa_lorentzian_summary.{pdf,png}` is separate."

## USER e126: "commit the WIP now, as well as the scintillation campaign"

Working tree now ALSO showed NEW modifications `budget_table.tex` + `foreground_table.tex` (weren't dirty before) — regenerated with "GENERATED FILE — do not edit by hand" provenance headers pointing to new emitter scripts (`galaxies/foreground/budget_table_emitter.py`, `foreground_table_emitter.py`, data in `budget_table_data.json`). Verified `--ignore-all-space`: **no numeric value changes**, only headers + whitespace normalization + (foreground) comment reflow.

Diffed WIP `results.tex`/`appendix.tex` vs origin/main: confirmed the small early hunks were **terminology reverts** of PR #30's polish (WIP predated it): main "dispersion-measure budget"→WIP "dispersion-budget"; "small dispersion-measure contribution"→"small dispersion"; "intervening DM"→"intervening dispersion"; appendix "dispersive delays"→"dispersion", "Intracluster dispersion measures"→"Intracluster dispersion", "hot-gas dispersion-measure columns"→"hot-gas dispersion columns", "dispersion measure for the four"→"dispersion for the four", "hot-baryon dispersion-measure column"→"hot-baryon dispersion column", "Forward-modeled host dispersion measure"→"Forward-modeled host dispersion", "host-DM posteriors"→"host-dispersion posteriors" (×2). Large hunks = genuine scintillation campaign additions (`fig:dsa_scint_gamma`, `app:dsa-scint-acf`, `\input{sections/dsa_scint_acf.tex}`).

**Splice solution:** took main's `results.tex`/`appendix.tex`, injected ONLY the additive scintillation content (results: swapped the scintillation subsection between `\subsection{Scintillation and screen attribution}` and `\FloatBarrier\subsection{Turbulence...`; appendix: appended the new `\section{DSA-110 scintillation ACF diagnostics}` to EOF). Verified each spliced file differs from main by a SINGLE additive hunk (results `@@ -114,21 +114,60`, appendix `@@ -150,3 +150,23`), zero terminology reverts, braces balanced (new_results 86/86, new_appendix 131/131, dsa_scint_acf.tex 35/35).

Submodule investigation: pointer bump `9017707`→`92b4fdf` — `92b4fdf` IS pushed to submodule remote `origin/agent/sightline-halo-grid-figure`. Submodule HEAD on branch `agent/sightline-halo-grid-figure`, has FURTHER uncommitted work (modified `crossmatching/plot_association_cards.py`, `analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py`, `galaxies/foreground/sightline_budget.py`, `uv.lock`, scint summary figures; untracked `budget_table_emitter.py`, `foreground_table_emitter.py`, `budget_table_data.json`, `foreground_table_data.json`, `test_budget_table_emitter.py`, `test_foreground_table_emitter.py`, refit configs/scripts, `exports/`, `scintillation/HANDOFF_SCINT_DATA_PRODUCTS.md`). Submodule scientific work committed onto main via PR #34 in the events immediately following this chunk (chunk ends at e140 reading submodule state).

The scintillation results text (from splice): DSA-band decorrelation-bandwidth γ from Lorentzian ACF fits in equal-S/N sub-bands, **41 clean components** across 12 co-detections, per-burst clean median γ spans ~0.24 MHz (FRB 20230913A) to ~23 MHz (FRB 20220310F), individual components ~0.13–23 MHz across 1.28–1.53 GHz DSA band, fixed γ∝ν⁴ guide for 9 bursts with ≥2 sub-band frequencies (single-freq: FRB 20230325A, FRB 20221113A, FRB 20220310F). CHIME-band campaign + two-band screen attribution deferred.

```
/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline
```

> Reminder from last time: a broken repo hook spams `fatal: Could not read from

> refs/heads/agent/sightline-halo-grid-figure`, not the push output.**

## Step 0 — orient (report back)

```bash
cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline
git rev-parse --short HEAD                    # expect 386e886
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD               # expect agent/sightline-halo-grid-figure
git status -s
```
If HEAD ≠ `386e886`, **stop and report**.

## Commit A — `plot_association_cards.py`: keep BOTH the style fix and the path fix

This file is `MM`. The staged half adds `use_flits_style()`; the unstaged
(worktree) half **reverts that line back** to `plt.style.use("default")` AND
adds the good path-de-hardcoding + `--manuscript-dir`/`--no-manuscript-copy`
argparse changes. **The author's decision: keep both fixes.** So take the
worktree content (path fix) but restore `use_flits_style()`.

Concretely — in the worktree file, **line 17** currently reads:

```python
plt.style.use("default")
```

Replace that single line with this block (mirrors exactly what the staged half
of this same file already contains — that half was authored against this tree,
so `from flits.plotting import use_flits_style` is the intended import; I could
**not** verify it resolves from my environment (no matplotlib there), so run the
`ast.parse` check below and, if you can, an actual import in the repo's venv):

```python
from flits.plotting import use_flits_style

# Adopt the shared FLITS style (Computer Modern serif, cmr10) so these cards
# match every other manuscript figure and the paper body font; then re-pin the
# card-specific small sizes and TrueType embedding the standard doesn't set.
use_flits_style()
```

Leave **all** the other worktree changes in place (the `DEFAULT_MANUSCRIPT_OUTDIR
= ROOT.parent / "figures" / "association_cards"` derivation, the two new
argparse flags, and the `copy_to_manuscript` guarding).

Then sanity-check and commit **just this file**:

```bash
python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('crossmatching/plot_association_cards.py').read()); print('parse OK')"
# optional but nice: python crossmatching/plot_association_cards.py --no-manuscript-copy  (should run without writing to the manuscript dir)

git add crossmatching/plot_association_cards.py
git commit -m "association cards: adopt shared FLITS style + derive manuscript dir from repo root

Keep the shared use_flits_style() (Computer Modern serif matching the paper
body) and replace the hardcoded Overleaf-sync output path
(/Users/.../overleaf/Faber2026/figures/association_cards, which lags main) with
DEFAULT_MANUSCRIPT_OUTDIR = ROOT.parent/figures/association_cards, resolved from
the file location so a fresh clone works. Add --manuscript-dir override and
--no-manuscript-copy for standalone submodule checkouts."
```

Confirm afterward: `git show --stat HEAD` lists exactly one file, and
`grep -n use_flits_style crossmatching/plot_association_cards.py` finds it.

---

## Commit B — `run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py`: RE-RUN first so figures match, then commit script + figures together

The script has been edited (font/PDF fix: `mathtext.fontset="stix"`,
`pdf.fonttype=42`, emit `.pdf`; plus a `--band dsa|chime` generalization), but
the tracked figures are stale — the script is newer than the committed
`.png`/`.svg`. **Do not commit the script without regenerating**, or the tracked
figures won't match the code.

```bash
cd analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07
# regenerate all DSA-band figures with the current script (default band = dsa)
python run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py            # adjust args if the script needs an input/config path — inspect --help first
cd -
git status -s analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/
```

Then stage the script **and** the regenerated tracked figures (26 tracked files
live in `results/figures/`: 12 per-burst `.png` + 12 per-burst `.svg` + summary
`.png` + `.svg`; note `*.pdf` is gitignored at .gitignore line 108, so PDFs
won't and shouldn't be added):

```bash
git add analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py \
        analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/figures/
git status -s   # confirm: script + only .png/.svg staged, no .pdf, no stragglers
git commit -m "DSA Lorentzian fits: fix mathtext/PDF fonts, add --band, regenerate figures

Use STIX mathtext + TrueType (pdf.fonttype=42) so the gamma glyph stops mapping
to U+00B0 and corrupting the PDF text layer; emit .pdf alongside png/svg. Add
--band dsa|chime. Per-burst output renames to {burst}_dsa_lorentzian_fits.* at
the default band. Regenerated all tracked figures to match the new code."
```

**Heads-up to flag if you hit it — orphan check (do NOT assume a specific
rename pattern).** The currently tracked per-burst figures are named
`{burst}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.{png,svg}` (12 bursts × 2 = 24 files, plus
`dsa_lorentzian_summary.{png,svg}`) — I verified this against `git ls-files`.
The `--band` generalization *may* change the output basename, but I have not
confirmed what the new script actually writes. So after the re-run, compare the
new output filenames against the tracked set:

```bash
cd analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/figures
echo "TRACKED:"; git ls-files . | sed 's#.*/##' | sort
echo "ON DISK:"; ls -1 *.png *.svg 2>/dev/null | sort
cd -
```

If the re-run wrote files under a *different* basename than the tracked ones,
the old tracked figures are now orphaned. **Report both lists rather than
deleting or `git rm`-ing anything** (no deletions per the ground rules); the
author will decide what to do with any orphans.

---

## Commit C — `uv.lock`: dependency resync (safe, its own commit)

Not incidental. `pyproject.toml` already declares `requires-python = ">=3.12"`,
so the committed lock was stale; regenerating collapses the per-Python duplicate
pins (116 → 108 packages; nothing dropped). Verify then commit:

```bash
uv lock --check          # expect exit 0 against the worktree lock
git add uv.lock
git commit -m "Resync uv.lock to requires-python>=3.12 (collapse per-Python duplicate pins)"
```
If `uv lock --check` does **not** exit 0, stop and report — do not commit.

---

## Commit D — refit configs/scripts + scint handoff doc (docs/config, its own commit)

`refit-2026-07-07/HANDOFF.md` is already committed and points at these files;
this closes that out. `.gitignore` already excludes the heavy/`.pyc` paths, so
adding the two dirs picks up exactly **8 YAML configs + 4 `.py` scripts** (the
`__pycache__/*.pyc` are correctly ignored). The scint handoff doc is docs-only.

```bash
git add analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/refit-2026-07-07/configs/ \
        analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/refit-2026-07-07/scripts/ \
        scintillation/HANDOFF_SCINT_DATA_PRODUCTS.md
git status -s   # confirm: 8 .yaml + 4 .py (refit_runner, refit_chunk, inspect_profiles, dump_plot) + 1 .md; NO .pyc
git commit -m "Add refit-2026-07-07 configs+scripts and scintillation data-products handoff

Ships the runner/chunk/inspect/dump scripts and 8 per-burst run configs that the
already-committed refit-2026-07-07/HANDOFF.md documents, plus the 174-line
scintillation data-products handoff. (The latter notes DATA_PROVENANCE.md is
stale on storage paths — h17:/data/jfaber/ was emptied ~2026-06-27, contents
moved to h17:/data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections/ — an open
follow-up, not addressed here.)"
```

---

## Step E — push and verify

```bash
git push origin agent/sightline-halo-grid-figure
git ls-remote origin refs/heads/agent/sightline-halo-grid-figure   # THIS is the source of truth
git status -sb                                                     # expect: up to date with origin/...
```

## Step F — report back

1. Step 0 output.
2. For **each** of commits A–D: the commit SHA (`git log --oneline -5`), and for
   B, C the sanity-check results (`ast.parse` / `uv lock --check` exit codes) and
   whether the figure re-run produced any orphaned old-named figures.
3. The final `git ls-remote` SHA after push.
4. Anything you stopped on or deviated from.

I need the **final HEAD SHA** (after all four commits) to bump the `Faber2026`
submodule pointer again — the pointer currently on `Faber2026` main is `386e886`,
so it will need a second bump to whatever these four commits produce.
